what_it_does: Folder constitution for bindings/ — the lenses of a variable instrument. Defines that one file per binding (bindings/<id>.py, file-discovered) declares a filling of the equation's slots (centre / angle_from / radius_from / k), states the instrument default resolves from raw data kinds, and specifies the BINDING row shape and the allowed values of angle_from.
observations: a BINDING is a filling of slots, NOT the sectors themselves — sectors resolve from angle_from against live data + registries
BINDING row schema given: {id, label, angle_from, radius_from, order_by?, groups?}
angle_from values: 'kind' (data-driven, one sector per distinct kind), 'kind-group' (declared groups, never the default), or a <registry-name>
states 'Nothing hardcoded is valid, only what occupies that variable at that point' — adding a lens = adding a file
attributes the instrument to 'Tim Geldard'; calls it 'Pure data (the floor)'
standouts: the variability principle: only what occupies the variable at that point is valid; no hardcoded sectors
adding a lens is adding a file (file-discovery), not editing code
angle_from over a <registry-name> resolves once the event->row edge is formalized
conventions: follows the repo's AGENTS.md folder-constitution convention
one row per file, file-discovered registry pattern
notes: Constitution for the lens registry: each bindings/<id>.py declares how to fill the instrument's equation slots; sectors are never hardcoded, they resolve from angle_from against live data and registries.
questions: What is 'k' in the equation slots and where is it set (the row schema lists no k field)?
What formalizing the 'event->row edge' entails before a <registry-name> angle_from resolves
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is a folder constitution and it defines the binding/lens model and row schema as stated.
apparent_themes: an instrument whose lenses are swappable data rows
intention_signals: Trying to establish that all lenses (sectors of a radial instrument) are data-driven fillings of a fixed equation — so the system grows by adding files, never by hardcoding sectors.
novelty: Framing UI lenses as algebraic slot-fillings (centre/angle_from/radius_from/k) of one equation, with a default that resolves from raw kinds.
contribution: Contributes the governing definition and row schema for the bindings/lens registry — what a binding is, the allowed angle_from sources, and the no-hardcoded-sectors law.
summary_for_embedding: The bindings/ folder constitution defining a binding as a file-discovered filling of an instrument equation's slots (centre/angle_from/radius_from/k) with the BINDING row schema and allowed angle_from values.
intention_for_embedding: To mandate that all lenses are data-driven slot-fillings resolved from live data and registries, added by file, never hardcoded.
